input.background: In an open (unblinded) trial of the beta-blocker alprenolol given to patients after myocardial infarction, randomization to alprenolol or the standard treatment was at the time of admission to hospital (Ahlmark and Saetre, 1976). The start of medication was two weeks after admission, by which time 60% of the original 393 patients had been withdrawn from the trial. Reasons for withdrawal were mostly death, non-confirmation of myocardial infarction or contraindication for the beta-blocker. Of the 162 patients actually treated, 69 received alprenolol and 93 the control treatment.
input.question: How could this problem have been avoided?

output.answer: By not allocating treatments (randomizing) until immediately before starting treatment.
